Title: Create a child location under an existing parent location
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with Manage Locations privilege; parent location "Main Hospital" exists
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Administration > Manage Locations | 2. Click Add Location | 3. Enter a unique name | 4. Select Main Hospital as parent location | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Child location is created and appears nested under Main Hospital in the location tree

Title: Verify multi-level hierarchy parent-of-parent resolves correctly
Preconditions: Locations Main Hospital > Ward A exist; Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Create location Bed 1 with parent Ward A | 2. Open Bed 1 details | 3. Inspect ancestor chain
Expected_Result: Bed 1 shows parent Ward A whose parent is Main Hospital; full three-level ancestry is resolvable

Title: Prevent assigning a location as its own parent
Preconditions: Location "Ward A" exists; Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Ward A for editing | 2. Set parent location to Ward A itself | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the save with a validation error preventing a self-referential parent

Title: Prevent creating a circular parent reference in the hierarchy
Preconditions: Locations Main Hospital > Ward A > Bed 1 exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open Main Hospital for editing | 2. Attempt to set its parent to Bed 1 (a descendant) | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the change preventing a cycle in the location hierarchy

Title: Reject duplicate location name within the same scope
Preconditions: Location "Ward A" already exists
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Location | 2. Enter name Ward A | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects creation with a duplicate-name validation message

Title: Location name is required and cannot be blank
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Location | 2. Leave Name empty | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error is shown and the location is not created

Title: Boundary check on location name maximum length (255 chars)
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Location | 2. Enter a 255-character name | 3. Save | 4. Repeat with 256 characters
Expected_Result: 255-char name is accepted; 256-char name is rejected or truncated per field limit

Title: Trim and reject whitespace-only location name
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Location | 2. Enter name as spaces only | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System trims input and rejects a whitespace-only name as blank

Title: Accept Unicode and accented characters in location name
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Location | 2. Enter name with accents/Unicode | 3. Save | 4. Reopen the saved location
Expected_Result: Name is stored and rendered correctly without corruption (UTF-8 round-trip)

Title: Reject script payload in location name (stored XSS)
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Location | 2. Enter a script tag payload as name | 3. Save | 4. View the location list and details
Expected_Result: Input is sanitized/encoded; no script executes when the name is rendered anywhere in the UI

Title: Tag a location with the Login Location tag
Preconditions: Login Location tag exists; Manage Locations privilege; location "Outpatient Clinic" exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit Outpatient Clinic | 2. Add tag Login Location | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Tag is associated; location now appears in the session/login location picker

Title: Login location picker lists only locations tagged Login Location
Preconditions: Two locations exist; only one tagged Login Location
Test_Steps: 1. Log out | 2. Authenticate with valid credentials | 3. Inspect the session location dropdown
Expected_Result: Only Login Location tagged locations appear; untagged locations are excluded from the picker

Title: Block login when no Login Location tagged locations exist
Preconditions: No location carries the Login Location tag
Test_Steps: 1. Log out | 2. Authenticate | 3. Observe session location step
Expected_Result: User cannot select a session location and is informed no login location is available

Title: Tag a location with Visit Location and start a visit there
Preconditions: Visit Location tag exists; patient with no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Tag Outpatient Clinic as Visit Location | 2. Start a visit selecting Outpatient Clinic | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Visit is created with the selected visit location and the location is persisted on the visit

Title: Visit location resolves to nearest ancestor tagged Visit Location
Preconditions: Hierarchy Main Hospital(Visit Location) > Ward A(untagged); patient on dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Start a visit at Ward A | 2. Inspect resolved visit location
Expected_Result: Visit location resolves up the tree to the nearest Visit Location tagged ancestor (Main Hospital)

Title: Pairwise tag combinations across login and visit tagging
Preconditions: Login Location and Visit Location tags exist
Test_Steps: 1. Create locations with tag combos: none / login-only / visit-only / both | 2. Verify each location's eligibility for login picker and visit selection
Expected_Result: Each combination behaves per its tags: login-eligibility and visit-eligibility match the assigned tags independently

Title: Create and assign a custom location tag
Preconditions: Manage Location Tags privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Manage Location Tags | 2. Add tag "Pharmacy Pickup" | 3. Assign it to a location | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Custom tag is created and successfully associated with the location

Title: Remove a tag from a location and confirm downstream effect
Preconditions: Outpatient Clinic tagged Login Location
Test_Steps: 1. Remove Login Location tag from Outpatient Clinic | 2. Log out and authenticate | 3. Inspect login location picker
Expected_Result: Location no longer appears in the login picker after the tag is removed

Title: Reject duplicate location tag name
Preconditions: Tag "Login Location" exists
Test_Steps: 1. Manage Location Tags | 2. Add tag named Login Location | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the duplicate tag with a validation message

Title: Define a custom location attribute type and set its value
Preconditions: Manage Location Attribute Types privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Add location attribute type "NPI" of free-text datatype | 2. Edit a location | 3. Set NPI value | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Attribute type is created and the value is stored against the location

Title: Validate attribute datatype enforcement (numeric/regex)
Preconditions: Location attribute type NPI with a 10-digit numeric constraint exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a location | 2. Enter a non-numeric NPI value | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the value with a datatype/format validation error

Title: Enforce maxOccurs=1 single-valued location attribute
Preconditions: Single-valued attribute type exists with a value already set
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a location with the attribute already set | 2. Add a second value for the same attribute | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System prevents a second value or replaces the existing value per single-valued rule

Title: Retiring an attribute type hides it from new entry but preserves history
Preconditions: Attribute type with existing values exists; Manage privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Retire the attribute type | 2. Open a location previously holding the value | 3. Open the new-attribute picker
Expected_Result: Retired type is hidden from new-entry pickers while historical values remain visible/preserved

Title: Retire an unused location with a required reason
Preconditions: Location "Old Storage" exists with no dependencies; Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Edit Old Storage | 2. Click Retire | 3. Enter a retire reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Location is retired, hidden from active pickers, and flagged retired with reason and retiring user recorded

Title: Retire requires a non-empty reason
Preconditions: Location exists; Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a location | 2. Click Retire | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System blocks retiring until a reason is provided

Title: Retired location is excluded from login and visit location pickers
Preconditions: A retired location that previously carried Login and Visit tags
Test_Steps: 1. Log out and authenticate | 2. Inspect login picker | 3. Start a visit and inspect location options
Expected_Result: Retired location does not appear in login or visit location selection lists

Title: Unretire a previously retired location restores availability
Preconditions: A retired location exists; Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Filter to include retired | 2. Open the retired location | 3. Click Unretire | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Location becomes active again, retire flag/reason cleared, and it reappears in applicable pickers

Title: Prevent or warn when retiring a location referenced by active visits
Preconditions: Location has at least one active visit referencing it
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to retire the location | 2. Observe system response
Expected_Result: System warns or blocks retiring a location still referenced by active clinical data, protecting referential integrity

Title: Retiring a parent location handling of child locations
Preconditions: Hierarchy Main Hospital > Ward A (both active)
Test_Steps: 1. Retire Main Hospital | 2. Inspect Ward A status and parent reference
Expected_Result: System defines behavior for children of a retired parent (cascade-warn or orphan-handling) consistently without breaking the tree

Title: Capture full structured address for a location
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege; address template configured
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a location | 2. Fill address1, city, state/province, postal code, country | 3. Save | 4. Reopen
Test_Data: Address1=10 Health St; City=Metropolis; State=NY; Postal=10001; Country=US
Expected_Result: All structured address fields persist and render in the configured address template order

Title: Postal code boundary and format validation
Preconditions: Address template with postal code field
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid postal code | 2. Enter an over-length/invalid postal code | 3. Save each
Expected_Result: Valid postal code is accepted; invalid/over-length input is rejected or constrained per template rules

Title: Address template field order reflects configured locale layout
Preconditions: Two address templates configured for different locales
Test_Steps: 1. Switch active address template | 2. Open the location address form | 3. Compare field order to template
Expected_Result: Address form field order and labels follow the active locale address template

Title: Reject control characters and injection in address fields
Preconditions: Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Enter address1 with SQL/script payload | 2. Save | 3. View location list and detail
Expected_Result: Input is parameterized/encoded; no injection occurs and stored value renders safely

Title: Set a system default location via global property
Preconditions: Manage Global Properties privilege; location exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit global property for default location | 2. Set it to a valid location | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Default location is stored and used as the system default where a location is not explicitly provided

Title: Default location pre-selects on visit/encounter forms
Preconditions: Default location configured to Outpatient Clinic
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new visit | 2. Inspect the pre-selected location field
Expected_Result: The location field defaults to the configured default location and remains editable

Title: Invalid/non-existent default location reference handling
Preconditions: Default location global property set to a deleted/invalid id
Test_Steps: 1. Set default location to an invalid reference | 2. Open a form expecting a default
Expected_Result: System handles the invalid reference gracefully (no crash) and prompts user to choose a location

Title: User personal default location overrides system default
Preconditions: System default set; user property default location set differently
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the user | 2. Open a location-defaulting form | 3. Inspect default
Expected_Result: User-level default takes precedence over the system default where supported

Title: Block location management without Manage Locations privilege
Preconditions: User without Manage Locations privilege is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate directly to Manage Locations URL | 2. Attempt to add/edit a location
Expected_Result: Access is denied with an authorization error; no create/edit is permitted

Title: Read-only role can view but not modify locations
Preconditions: Role with view-only location access
Test_Steps: 1. Open the location list | 2. Attempt to edit a location
Expected_Result: Locations are viewable but edit/retire controls are disabled or blocked

Title: Location create/edit/retire are written to the audit log
Preconditions: Auditing enabled; Manage Locations privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Create, then edit, then retire a location | 2. Query the audit/changelog
Expected_Result: Each action is recorded with actor, timestamp, and changed fields in the audit trail

Title: Location uuid is unique and immutable across edits
Preconditions: DB access to the location table; a location exists
Test_Steps: 1. Note the location uuid | 2. Edit the location name | 3. Re-query the uuid
Expected_Result: uuid remains unchanged after edits and is globally unique in the location table

Title: Referential integrity between location and location_tag_map
Preconditions: DB access; a tagged location exists
Test_Steps: 1. Delete a tag mapping row directly | 2. Reload the location | 3. Verify orphan handling
Expected_Result: FK relationship is enforced; no orphaned tag mappings persist after the location/tag is removed

Title: Retrieve a Location resource by id via FHIR R4
Preconditions: FHIR module enabled; an OpenMRS location maps to a FHIR Location id
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Location/{id} with valid auth | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with a valid FHIR R4 Location resource containing id, name, and status

Title: Search Location by name returns a Bundle
Preconditions: FHIR module enabled; locations exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Location?name=Clinic | 2. Inspect the searchset Bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with a searchset Bundle whose entries match the name parameter

Title: Retired OpenMRS location maps to FHIR Location.status=inactive
Preconditions: A retired location exists; FHIR enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Location for the retired location | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: FHIR Location.status is inactive for the retired location, mirroring OpenMRS retire state

Title: FHIR Location.partOf reflects the parent location hierarchy
Preconditions: Hierarchy Main Hospital > Ward A; FHIR enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Location for Ward A | 2. Inspect partOf reference
Expected_Result: Location.partOf references the parent location resource matching the OpenMRS hierarchy

Title: FHIR Location address maps from structured OpenMRS address fields
Preconditions: Location with full address; FHIR enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Location | 2. Inspect the address element
Expected_Result: FHIR Location.address line/city/state/postalCode/country map correctly from OpenMRS address fields

Title: Reject FHIR Location create with invalid/unsupported status code
Preconditions: FHIR write enabled; authorized client
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Location resource with status=bogus | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 4xx with an OperationOutcome rejecting the invalid status value

Title: Unauthenticated FHIR Location request is rejected
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint requires authentication
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Location/{id} with no credentials | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; no Location data is returned

Title: HL7 message facility/location field maps to an OpenMRS location
Preconditions: HL7 inbound configured; mapping for facility code exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT message with MSH/PV1 facility-location populated | 2. Process the message | 3. Inspect the resulting encounter location
Expected_Result: Inbound location is resolved to the mapped OpenMRS location on the created encounter/visit

Title: Unmapped HL7 facility code is rejected or queued for review
Preconditions: HL7 inbound configured; facility code with no mapping
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT message with an unknown facility code | 2. Inspect processing outcome
Expected_Result: Message errors out or is queued for manual mapping rather than silently assigning a wrong location

Title: Manage Locations form is keyboard navigable with labels
Preconditions: Screen reader/keyboard testing setup; Manage Locations page open
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all form fields | 2. Verify focus order and visible focus | 3. Verify each field has an associated label
Expected_Result: All fields are reachable by keyboard in logical order with visible focus and programmatic labels

Title: Location validation errors are announced to assistive tech
Preconditions: Screen reader active; Manage Locations form open
Test_Steps: 1. Submit the form with a blank name | 2. Observe the validation error announcement
Expected_Result: Validation error is associated with the field and announced via aria-live/role=alert

Title: Deep hierarchy depth performance and rendering boundary
Preconditions: Ability to create a deep chain of nested locations
Test_Steps: 1. Create a chain 10 levels deep | 2. Open the location tree | 3. Expand to the leaf
Expected_Result: Tree renders and resolves ancestry without error or unacceptable lag at depth

Title: Exploratory probing of tag-driven behavior edge cases
Preconditions: Login and Visit tags exist; mixed tagged/retired locations
Test_Steps: 1. Combine retire + tag states | 2. Toggle tags on parents vs children | 3. Observe picker and visit-resolution behavior across combinations
Expected_Result: No inconsistent states surface; tag-driven pickers and visit resolution behave predictably across edge combinations

Title: Pairwise of role x default-source x form determines effective location
Preconditions: System and user defaults configured; multiple roles
Test_Steps: 1. For each role/default-source/form combination, open the form | 2. Record the effective pre-selected location
Expected_Result: Effective location follows the documented precedence consistently across the sampled combinations
